1 - Lagging: Design deliverables frequently miss deadlines; Figma artboards lack version control and organization.
2 - Developing: Design deliverables occasionally delayed; inconsistent use of Figma components and layers across team.
3 - Proficient: Design deliverables meet timelines consistently; Figma workspace organized with clear naming conventions and component libraries.
4 - Exemplary: Design deliverables exceed expected velocity; Figma auto-layout and component systems enable rapid iteration and team scaling.

1 - Lagging: Estimation of design cycles is unreliable; team workload fluctuates unpredictably.
2 - Developing: Design timeline estimates are sometimes accurate; workload distribution occasionally uneven across UI/UX and multimedia teams.
3 - Proficient: Design cycles are predictable with 80%+ accuracy; team capacity planning aligns with project demands.
4 - Exemplary: Design cycle predictions are highly accurate; proactive capacity management prevents bottlenecks and supports strategic initiatives.

1 - Lagging: Design inconsistencies across screens; Adobe files lack color standards and typography guidelines.
2 - Developing: Design inconsistencies occur occasionally; partial adherence to design system tokens and accessibility standards.
3 - Proficient: Designs maintain system-wide consistency; color palettes, typography, and spacing follow documented standards in Figma.
4 - Exemplary: Designs exemplify design excellence; comprehensive design system with fully tokenized components, accessibility compliance, and cross-platform consistency.

1 - Lagging: Limited collaboration between UI/UX, multimedia, and research; design handoffs to development lack clarity.
2 - Developing: Design teams collaborate inconsistently; handoff documentation to dev is sometimes incomplete.
3 - Proficient: Regular cross-functional design reviews; clear handoff documentation includes specs, Figma links, and design rationale.
4 - Exemplary: Seamless cross-team collaboration; design-to-dev handoff includes interactive prototypes, design tokens, and clear asset organization.

Rating: Team Allocation (UI/UX vs Multimedia vs Research)
1 - Lagging: Misalignment between staffing and project needs; UI/UX designers underutilized while multimedia team backlogged.
2 - Developing: Resource allocation sometimes reactive; occasional conflicts between UI/UX, multimedia, and research priorities.
3 - Proficient: Team allocation aligns with strategic projects; UI/UX, multimedia, and research teams work synergistically.
4 - Exemplary: Optimal resource allocation; dynamic staffing model maximizes utilization of 9 UI/UX and 3 research/multimedia staff across initiatives.

1 - Lagging: Team consistently overloaded; design debt accumulates due to capacity constraints.
2 - Developing: Capacity planning exists but is reactive; design sprints sometimes overcommitted.
3 - Proficient: Proactive capacity planning; team operates at sustainable utilization levels with buffer for emerging priorities.
4 - Exemplary: Data-driven capacity forecasting; workload balanced to maintain quality while enabling design innovation and team development.

1 - Lagging: Figma, Adobe Suite, ClickUp underutilized; design file management chaotic.
2 - Developing: Teams use tools inconsistently; partial adoption of design system in Figma; ClickUp lacks design project visibility.
3 - Proficient: Figma teams share component libraries; Adobe Suite integrated into workflows; ClickUp tracks design milestones.
4 - Exemplary: Best-in-class design infrastructure; Figma with multi-file libraries and version control; Adobe automation workflows; ClickUp fully integrated for design tracking.

1 - Lagging: Inefficient spending on design tools and software; redundant tool subscriptions.

3 - Proficient: Budget allocated strategically across tools, training, and design assets; spending aligns with team needs.
4 - Exemplary: Optimized budget allocation; leverages volume licensing, identifies cost-saving opportunities, maximizes ROI on design investments.

4 - Exemplary: Proactive stakeholder engagement; design decisions documented with rationale; design thinking communicated across organization.
